steps.title: Find the rectangle diagonal
steps.content:
An inscribed rectangle has a diagonal equal to the circle's diameter. From the circumference,
$$
118\pi=2\pi r
$$
$$
r=59
$$
So the diameter, and the rectangle diagonal, is $118$.

steps.title: Find the side lengths
steps.content:
The diagonal is twice the shortest side, so the shortest side is
$$
\frac{118}{2}=59
$$
Let the longer side be $b$. Use the Pythagorean theorem.
$$
59^2+b^2=118^2
$$
$$
b^2=118^2-59^2=3(59^2)
$$
$$
b=59\sqrt{3}
$$

steps.title: Multiply the dimensions
steps.content: The rectangle area is $$ 59(59\sqrt{3})=3{,}481\sqrt{3} $$ The answer is D.
